name: White Pork Sausage (Boudin Blanc De Liege)
recipeIngredient: 1 large Celery stalk with leaves, chopped
12 ounces Boneless Lean Pork Center Loin, trimmed of all fat and cut into 1-inch chunks
9 ounces Fresh Pork Fat (from loin), fresh bacon or fatback, well chilled
9 Feet Sausage Casings, rinsed inside and out, soaked in cold water for 1 hour, then rinsed again
recipeInstructions:
1. In a heavy 4-quart saucepan, combine milk, chopped onions, carrots, celery, parsley, 3 shallots, garlic clove, bay leaves, salt, cloves, pepper, nutmeg, and crumbled thyme. Bring to a boil over medium heat.
2. Remove from heat and let stand for 30 minutes.
3. Refrigerate until the mixture is well chilled, about 2 hours.
4. Meanwhile, melt 1 tablespoon butter in a heavy small skillet over low heat. Add the remaining 4 minced shallots. Cover and cook until very soft, stirring occasionally, about 15 minutes.
5. Puree with on/off turns in a food processor with pork, pork fat, eggs, flour, Tawny Port, and 1/4 teaspoon garlic until smooth.
6. Strain milk into the processor, pressing down on solids to extract as much liquid as possible. Blend into puree.
7. Transfer to a large bowl. Stir in currants. Refrigerate for 1 to 8 hours (if the processor has small capacity, puree in batches).
8. Cut sausage casings into 3-foot lengths and tie knots at one end of each.
9. Gather one piece around the tip of a pastry bag fitted with a 1/2-inch plain tip. Spoon pork mixture into the pastry bag, pressing down to prevent air pockets.
10. Pipe mixture into casing, twisting the casing every 6 inches to create individual sausages. Tie a knot at the end of the casing. Repeat with remaining pork and casings.
11. Tie twists in sausage with string. Bring 8 quarts water to a boil in a stockpot. Remove from heat and add sausages.
12. Return pot to heat, adjusting as necessary to maintain the water just below simmer (180°F). Cook until sausages are firm to touch, about 20 minutes, turning occasionally.
13. Cool in cold water to retain plumpness. Cut into individual links.
14. Sausage can be prepared ahead to this point, wrapped and refrigerated up to 3 days or frozen up to 2 months.
15. Melt butter in a heavy skillet over medium heat. Lightly brown sausages on all sides for about 8 minutes total. Serve hot (sausage can also be grilled).
